title: leptosiphon pygmaeus
intro: Leptosiphon pygmaeus, also known as Pygmy Blue Eyed Mary, is a rare and endangered annual herbaceous plant native to the coastal scrub and chaparral regions of California in the United States. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its bioactive compounds such as flavonoids and phenolic acids. Leptosiphon pygmaeus has been used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and skin conditions, due to its antipyretic, expectorant, and antimicrobial properties. Herbal preparations of Leptosiphon pygmaeus are typically made from its leaves and flowers, which can be used in teas, infusions, or tinctures to harness its medicinal benefits.
